safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_hub 2026-06-24 — optimal tap ~$1,000 at the bottom of the $1,000–1,200 demand zone (monthly fair-value gap + 11-month Bollinger band + monthly value-area-high + round number)
safest_plan.stop_basis: zero_hub 2026-06-24 — a monthly close below the 11-month Bollinger band (which anchors the ~$1,000 zone); ~950 is an estimate of where a close-below would sit (estimated, no explicit number given)

stance_by_source.summary: Planned long from the $1,000–1,200 demand zone after a roughly -45/50% drop; best around $1,000, but price hasn't reached the zone yet and a monthly confirmation is still required before entering.

plain_english: Only one source covers palladium, and it is bullish — it wants to buy from a deep support area around $1,000 (the band runs $1,000 to $1,200) after the metal fell roughly 45-50%, aiming for $1,500 and then $1,727. The reward-to-risk is good (about 2.6 to 1, rising to 4 to 1). But this is a plan, not a live trade: the price has not yet dropped into the buy zone, and the source is waiting for the monthly chart to confirm before committing. Do not buy yet — wait for price to reach roughly $1,000 and for a monthly confirmation; if the monthly candle closes below that zone (around $950), the idea is dead.
